title: Complete Lesson Plan Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a structured lesson plan with objectives, activities, timing, and assessment.
prompt:
Role: You are an experienced teacher and curriculum designer.

Task: Create a [DURATION] lesson plan on [TOPIC] for [GRADE/LEVEL], aligned to [CURRICULUM/STANDARD if any]. Keep it practical.

Format: 3 measurable objectives; a hook/warm-up; a time-boxed activity sequence (teacher & student actions); one group and one individual task; differentiation for struggling and advanced learners; a formative assessment + exit ticket; required materials.

title: Active-Recall Study Quiz
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns notes or a topic into a spaced, active-recall quiz with explanations.
prompt:
Role: You are a learning scientist who designs retrieval practice.

Task: Create an active-recall quiz on [TOPIC or paste notes] for [LEVEL], ordered easy to hard.

Format: 12 questions — 5 multiple-choice, 4 short-answer, 3 'explain why'. Put answers with brief explanations below a divider. End with the 3 concepts to review again. Notes/topic: [PASTE HERE].

title: Personalized Study / Learning Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a realistic week-by-week roadmap to learn a skill, with resources and milestones.
prompt:
Role: You are a learning coach.

Task: Build a study plan to learn [SKILL/SUBJECT] from [CURRENT LEVEL] to [GOAL LEVEL] in [TIMEFRAME] at [HOURS]/week. Front-load fundamentals.

Format: A week-by-week roadmap (focus topic, outcomes, resource types, a small applied project, a checkpoint). End with the 3 beginner mistakes to avoid in this area.

title: Explain With the Perfect Analogy
author: PromptDeck
description: Explains a concept using tailored analogies from a field you already understand.
prompt:
Role: You are a master explainer.

Task: Explain [CONCEPT] to me using analogies drawn from [FIELD I KNOW, e.g. cooking, football, music]. Keep the analogy accurate and note where it breaks down.

Format: A one-line plain definition; the extended analogy; a mapping of each analogy part to the real concept; and where the analogy stops being true.

title: Socratic Tutor (Learn by Questions)
author: PromptDeck
description: Tutors you on a topic by asking guiding questions instead of just giving answers.
prompt:
Role: You are a patient Socratic tutor.

Task: Teach me [TOPIC] at [LEVEL] by asking one guiding question at a time and adapting to my answers, only explaining directly when I'm stuck. Correct misconceptions gently.

Format: Start with a quick diagnostic question, then proceed one question at a time. After I answer, acknowledge, correct if needed, and ask the next. Begin now with question 1.

title: Grading Rubric Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a clear, fair grading rubric for an assignment with performance levels.
prompt:
Role: You are an assessment specialist.

Task: Create a grading rubric for the assignment [ASSIGNMENT] at [LEVEL], worth [POINTS/WEIGHT], measuring [KEY SKILLS/CRITERIA].

Format: A rubric table — Criterion | Excellent | Proficient | Developing | Beginning | Weight. Keep level descriptions concrete and observable. Add a one-line note on how to give useful feedback with it.

title: Spaced-Repetition Flashcards
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns material into clean question/answer flashcards for Anki-style review.
prompt:
Role: You are a memory coach who builds effective flashcards.

Task: Create flashcards from the material below on [TOPIC] for [LEVEL]. Follow good card design — one fact per card, clear cue, no ambiguity.

Format: 15 cards as 'Q | A' rows suitable for import (pipe-separated), ordered from foundational to advanced. Material: [PASTE MATERIAL].

title: Essay / Writing Feedback Coach
author: PromptDeck
description: Gives structured feedback on an essay covering thesis, structure, evidence, and style.
prompt:
Role: You are a writing tutor.

Task: Give feedback on the essay below written for [ASSIGNMENT/LEVEL]. Be encouraging but specific; don't rewrite it for me — coach me to improve it.

Format: (1) 2 strengths; (2) feedback on thesis, structure, evidence, and style (each with one concrete suggestion); (3) 3 priority fixes; (4) a sample improved topic sentence. Essay: [PASTE ESSAY].

title: Concept Map / Topic Breakdown
author: PromptDeck
description: Breaks a big topic into a structured concept map showing how ideas connect.
prompt:
Role: You are an instructional designer.

Task: Break down [TOPIC] for [LEVEL] into a concept map showing main branches, sub-concepts, and how they connect.

Format: An indented outline (main topic → branches → sub-concepts), then a short list of the key relationships between branches, and the recommended order to learn them.

title: Generate Practice Problems + Solutions
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates graded practice problems with worked solutions for any subject.
prompt:
Role: You are a subject tutor.

Task: Create practice problems on [TOPIC] for [LEVEL], ranging easy to challenging, that reinforce [SPECIFIC SKILL].

Format: 8 problems ordered by difficulty. Below a divider, provide fully worked step-by-step solutions and a common mistake to avoid for each.

title: Explain a Research Paper Simply
author: PromptDeck
description: Summarizes a dense paper or abstract into plain language with key takeaways.
prompt:
Role: You are a science communicator.

Task: Explain the paper/abstract below in plain language for a [LEVEL] reader, keeping the findings accurate and noting limitations.

Format: (1) the core question; (2) what they did (method) simply; (3) what they found; (4) why it matters; (5) caveats/limitations; (6) 3 terms defined. Paper/abstract: [PASTE HERE].

title: Course / Curriculum Outline
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a full course outline with modules, outcomes, and assessments.
prompt:
Role: You are a curriculum architect.

Task: Design a course on [SUBJECT] for [AUDIENCE/LEVEL], lasting [DURATION], with the end goal that learners can [OUTCOME].

Format: Course description & learning outcomes; a module-by-module outline (module → topics → outcome → activity); assessments and a capstone; required resources; and a suggested pacing schedule.

title: Tailor My Resume to a Job
author: PromptDeck
description: Rewrites resume bullets to match a job description, optimized for ATS and impact.
prompt:
Role: You are a professional resume writer and ATS expert.

Task: Tailor my resume to the job below. Keep everything truthful to my input.

Format: (1) the top 10 keywords/skills the job emphasizes; (2) rewritten experience bullets in 'action verb + task + quantified result' form mirroring that language; (3) gaps between my resume and the job with how to address them. Job: [PASTE JD]. Resume: [PASTE RESUME].

title: Mock Interview & Answer Coach
author: PromptDeck
description: Runs a role-specific mock interview and coaches your answers using STAR.
prompt:
Role: You are a hiring manager interviewing me for a [ROLE].

Task: Run a mock interview. Ask one question at a time (behavioral + role-specific). After each answer, give feedback: what was strong, what to improve, and a tighter STAR-structured version.

Format: First list 8 likely questions for this role, then begin with question 1 and proceed one at a time. Job context: [PASTE JD/ROLE].

title: Tailored Cover Letter
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a concise, specific cover letter matched to a job and your background.
prompt:
Role: You are a career writer.

Task: Write a cover letter for the job below using my background. Under 250 words, 3 short paragraphs, no clichés.

Format: Open with a specific hook (not 'I am writing to apply'); connect 2–3 of my achievements to their needs with a metric; match their tone; close confidently and low-pressure. Job: [PASTE JD]. Background: [PASTE HIGHLIGHTS].

title: LinkedIn Headline & About Rewrite
author: PromptDeck
description: Rewrites your LinkedIn headline and About to be keyword-rich and magnetic to recruiters.
prompt:
Role: You are a personal-branding strategist.

Task: Rewrite my LinkedIn headline and About. My goal: [ROLE/WHAT I WANT TO BE HIRED FOR]; achievements: [LIST]; audience: [RECRUITERS/CLIENTS].

Format: (1) 3 headline options (≤220 chars, value-led, keyword-rich); (2) a first-person About — hook, what I do & for whom, proof with metrics, 3–4 searchable keywords, a soft CTA. Confident, not braggy.

title: Salary Negotiation Coach & Scripts
author: PromptDeck
description: Prepares your case and gives scripts to negotiate a job offer confidently.
prompt:
Role: You are a compensation negotiation coach.

Task: Help me negotiate an offer for [ROLE] at [COMPANY]. Current offer: [OFFER]; my target: [TARGET]; my leverage: [OTHER OFFERS/SKILLS]. Keep it collaborative and professional.

Format: My value-based justification; a target/anchor/walk-away; word-for-word scripts to counter on base, and to negotiate non-salary items (equity, PTO, remote, start date); and how to respond if they say no.

title: Career Development Roadmap
author: PromptDeck
description: Maps a realistic path from your current role to a target role with skills and milestones.
prompt:
Role: You are a career coach.

Task: Build a roadmap from my current role [CURRENT] to target role [TARGET] over [TIMEFRAME], given my strengths [STRENGTHS] and gaps [GAPS].

Format: The skills/experiences to acquire; a phased milestone plan (e.g. 3/6/12 months); projects or roles to seek; people/networks to build; and 3 signals that show I'm ready for the next step.

title: Professional Resignation Letter
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a gracious, bridge-preserving resignation letter.
prompt:
Role: You are a professional communication advisor.

Task: Write a resignation letter for my role [ROLE] at [COMPANY], last day [DATE], reason [OPTIONAL/BRIEF]. Keep it gracious and burn no bridges.

Format: A concise letter — clear statement of resignation and last day, brief thanks and a positive note, an offer to help with transition, and a warm close. Keep it professional regardless of my true feelings.

title: Networking / Referral Outreach Message
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a genuine networking message to ask for advice, a chat, or a referral.
prompt:
Role: You are a networking coach.

Task: Write a message to [PERSON/ROLE] at [COMPANY] to [ASK ADVICE / REQUEST A CHAT / ASK FOR A REFERRAL], given my connection to them [CONTEXT]. Warm, specific, low-pressure — give a reason it's them.

Format: A short message (≤110 words) with a personalized opener, a clear and easy ask, and gratitude. Then a shorter LinkedIn-DM version and a follow-up if no reply.

title: Self-Review / Performance Write-Up
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns your accomplishments into a strong, evidence-based performance self-review.
prompt:
Role: You are a career coach who writes compelling self-reviews.

Task: Write my performance self-review for the period [PERIOD] in role [ROLE], based on accomplishments [LIST] and goals [GOALS]. Be confident and specific, backed by impact.

Format: Key accomplishments (with metrics/impact); how I lived the company values/competencies; growth areas framed constructively; goals for next period. Keep it honest and evidence-based.

title: Professional Self-Introduction
author: PromptDeck
description: Crafts a confident self-introduction for interviews, networking, or events.
prompt:
Role: You are a communication coach.

Task: Write a self-introduction for me for context [INTERVIEW / NETWORKING EVENT / NEW TEAM], given my background [BACKGROUND] and goal [GOAL]. Natural and confident, not a résumé readout.

Format: A 30-second spoken version and a 10-second version. Include who I am, what I do/value, a proof point, and a hook that invites conversation.

title: Write a LinkedIn Recommendation
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a specific, credible recommendation for a colleague or report.
prompt:
Role: You are a thoughtful manager/colleague.

Task: Write a LinkedIn recommendation for [NAME], who was my [RELATIONSHIP], highlighting strengths [STRENGTHS] and a specific example [EXAMPLE]. Make it credible and warm, not generic.

Format: A short recommendation — how I know them, 2–3 specific strengths with a concrete example, their impact, and a confident closing endorsement.

title: Portfolio Project Case Study
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a project into a portfolio case study that shows your thinking and impact.
prompt:
Role: You are a portfolio coach.

Task: Write a case study for my project [PROJECT], my role [ROLE], the problem [PROBLEM], what I did [ACTIONS], and the outcome [RESULT]. Show my thinking, not just the output.

Format: Overview & my role; the problem & constraints; my process and key decisions; the solution; the result/impact (with metrics); what I learned. Keep it skimmable with clear headings.

title: Personal Budget & Savings Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a simple monthly budget and savings plan (general educational guidance).
prompt:
Role: You are a budgeting coach. (General educational guidance, not professional financial advice.)

Task: Build a monthly budget from income (after tax) [AMOUNT], fixed costs [RENT, BILLS], and goals [SAVE FOR X / PAY OFF DEBT].

Format: A suggested budget as a table using a clear framework (e.g. 50/30/20) adapted to my numbers; a realistic monthly savings target; 3 likely areas to cut; and a priority order (emergency fund, high-interest debt, etc.).

title: Weekly Meal Plan & Grocery List
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a balanced weekly meal plan and grocery list (general wellness guidance).
prompt:
Role: You are a meal-planning assistant. (General wellness guidance, not medical/dietary advice.)

Task: Create a 7-day meal plan for goal [WEIGHT LOSS/MUSCLE/MAINTENANCE], preferences/restrictions [VEGETARIAN/ALLERGIES], ~[CALORIES] per day, cooking time [MINUTES]. Keep ingredients affordable and reused across days.

Format: Breakfast, lunch, dinner, and a snack per day; then a consolidated grocery list grouped by aisle. Note that anyone with medical conditions should consult a professional.

title: Beginner-Friendly Workout Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Designs a weekly workout routine for your goal, level, and equipment (general fitness guidance).
prompt:
Role: You are a fitness coach. (General fitness guidance, not medical advice — consult a professional before starting.)

Task: Design a weekly plan for goal [STRENGTH/FAT LOSS/GENERAL], level [BEGINNER/INTERMEDIATE], [N] days/week, equipment [HOME/GYM/NONE], [MINUTES]/session.

Format: A day-by-day split with exercises, sets/reps, and rest; a warm-up and cool-down; weekly progression; form cues for main lifts; and swap options for limited mobility.

title: Habit-Building Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a goal into a realistic habit plan using proven behavior-change tactics.
prompt:
Role: You are a behavior-change coach.

Task: Help me build the habit [HABIT] toward goal [GOAL], given my obstacles [OBSTACLES] and daily routine [ROUTINE]. Use habit-stacking, tiny starts, and cue design.

Format: A specific implementation intention ('after [existing habit], I will [tiny version]'); an environment tweak; a weekly ramp; how to track it; a plan for missed days (never miss twice); and one reward.

title: Travel Itinerary Planner
author: PromptDeck
description: Plans a day-by-day trip itinerary tailored to your interests, budget, and pace.
prompt:
Role: You are a well-traveled trip planner.

Task: Plan a [DAYS]-day trip to [DESTINATION] for [TRAVELERS], with interests [INTERESTS], budget [BUDGET], and pace [RELAXED/PACKED]. Group activities by area to reduce backtracking.

Format: A day-by-day itinerary (morning/afternoon/evening) with activities, rough costs, and food suggestions; a packing note; and 3 local tips. Flag that opening hours/prices should be verified.

title: Thoughtful Gift Idea Finder
author: PromptDeck
description: Suggests personalized gift ideas across budgets based on the person and occasion.
prompt:
Role: You are a thoughtful gift concierge.

Task: Suggest gifts for [PERSON: relationship, age, interests, personality] for occasion [OCCASION], budget [BUDGET]. Avoid generic clichés; match their actual interests.

Format: 10 ideas grouped by budget (under/around/splurge), each with a one-line why-it-fits. Then 2 experience-based (non-object) options and 1 DIY/personal idea.

title: Script a Difficult Conversation
author: PromptDeck
description: Helps you prepare and phrase a hard personal or work conversation with empathy.
prompt:
Role: You are a communication coach skilled in nonviolent communication.

Task: Help me approach a difficult conversation with [WHO] about [ISSUE], where I want [DESIRED OUTCOME] and I'm worried about [FEAR]. Keep it respectful and non-blaming.

Format: How to open; observation-feeling-need-request phrasing; 2–3 things to actually say; how to handle a defensive reaction; and what to avoid saying. Keep it calm and human.

title: Personalized Journaling Prompts
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates reflective journaling prompts tailored to what you're working through.
prompt:
Role: You are a reflective journaling guide. (Supportive, not a substitute for professional mental-health care.)

Task: Create journaling prompts to help me explore [THEME, e.g. stress, clarity, gratitude, a decision], at depth [GENTLE/DEEP].

Format: 10 prompts grouped as Reflect / Understand / Move forward, each open-ended and non-judgmental. Add one 2-minute quick prompt for busy days.

title: Home Declutter & Organize Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a room-by-room decluttering plan you can actually finish.
prompt:
Role: You are a professional organizer.

Task: Create a decluttering plan for [SPACE/HOME], with time available [TIME], and challenge [SENTIMENTAL ITEMS / SMALL SPACE / etc.]. Make it doable in small sessions.

Format: A room-by-room order (easiest wins first); a per-session checklist; a simple keep/donate/toss decision rule; storage suggestions; and a maintenance habit to stop it rebuilding.

title: Speech / Toast Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a heartfelt, well-paced speech or toast for a personal occasion.
prompt:
Role: You are a speechwriter for personal occasions.

Task: Write a [LENGTH] speech/toast for [OCCASION] about [PERSON/RELATIONSHIP], including these memories/qualities [DETAILS], with tone [HEARTFELT/FUNNY/BOTH]. Written to be spoken aloud.

Format: An opening that grabs attention; 1–2 stories or qualities; a heartfelt turn; a memorable closing line/toast. Add a note on pacing and where to pause.
